title: Join Execution Algorithms
learning_outcomes: Describe structural characteristics of Join Execution Algorithms.
Derive mathematical and programmatic bounds for Nested Loop Joins, Block Nested Loop, Index Nested Loop, Sort-Merge Join, and Hash Join.

content_sections.title: Join Execution Algorithms
content_sections.content:
### DBMS Architecture and Transaction Execution
Databases operate on structured page models. The storage engine coordinates buffer pools and physical reads/writes, while the query processor compiles relational algebra into execution plans.

#### Slotted Page Offset Math
Records are of variable length. The page header stores slot directory arrays mapping slot index to page offsets:
$$\text{Offset}_{i} = \text{PageSize} - \sum_{k=1}^i \text{Len}(R_k)$$
This layout allows page defragmentation (shuffling records and updating offset vectors) without changing record IDs (RIDs) which represent (PageNum, SlotNum).

#### B+ Tree Node Fan-out
Leaves contain data, and internal nodes store search keys. The maximum fan-out $F$ of an internal node on a page of size $P$ with key size $K$ and pointer size $D$ is bounded by:
$$F \times D + (F - 1) \times K \le P \implies F \le \frac{P + K}{D + K}$$

#### ARIES Recovery Logic
ARIES recovery logs all database changes using Write-Ahead Logging (WAL) and executes three recovery phases:
1.  **Analysis Phase:** Scans WAL forward from the last checkpoint to rebuild the Transaction Table and Dirty Page Table (DPT).
2.  **Redo Phase:** Replays history forward from the minimum `RecLSN` in the DPT to restore the state before crash.
3.  **Undo Phase:** Rolls back uncommitted transactions active at the time of crash by scanning backwards using `PrevLSN` pointers and logging Compensation Log Records (CLRs) to prevent infinite loops during nested aborts.

### Depth Analysis: Join Execution Algorithms
In production configurations, we analyze how Nested Loop Joins, Block Nested Loop, Index Nested Loop, Sort-Merge Join, and Hash Join affects performance parameters. We evaluate memory layouts and consensus thresholds.

callouts.title: Common Trap in Join Execution Algorithms
callouts.content: A common mistake when analyzing Join Execution Algorithms is ignoring latency bounds and boundary edge cases during concurrent executions.

callouts.title: Production Scaling for Join Execution Algorithms
callouts.content: Production servers implement strict resource isolation and cache alignments to prevent performance regressions when running Join Execution Algorithms loops.
